title: Kangaroo Level 1-2 - Practice Set 2: Shapes & Patterns
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: How many triangles do you see in the picture?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 300 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0' font-family='sans-serif'><polygon points='20,80 60,80 40,40'/><polygon points='80,80 120,80 100,40'/><polygon points='140,80 180,80 160,40'/><polygon points='200,80 240,80 220,40'/></svg>
questions.solution: Count each triangle: there are 4 triangles in a row.

questions.question_text: Mia has 7 stickers. She gives 3 to her friend. How many stickers does Mia have now?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 280 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='10' y='15' width='25' height='30'/><rect x='40' y='15' width='25' height='30'/><rect x='70' y='15' width='25' height='30'/><rect x='100' y='15' width='25' height='30'/><rect x='130' y='15' width='25' height='30'/><rect x='160' y='15' width='25' height='30'/><rect x='190' y='15' width='25' height='30'/><text x='230' y='35' font-size='14' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>-3</text></svg>
questions.solution: 7 minus 3 equals 4 stickers remaining.

questions.question_text: Which shape has exactly 4 equal sides?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 300 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0' font-family='sans-serif'><polygon points='10,60 40,10 70,60'/><rect x='90' y='15' width='45' height='45'/><rect x='155' y='20' width='60' height='35'/><circle cx='250' cy='40' r='22'/><text x='22' y='75' font-size='10'>A</text><text x='108' y='75' font-size='10'>B</text><text x='180' y='75' font-size='10'>C</text><text x='245' y='75' font-size='10'>D</text></svg>
questions.solution: A square has 4 sides that are all equal in length.

questions.question_text: How many stars are shown?
questions.solution: Count each star: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5.

questions.question_text: Which number comes next in the pattern: 2, 4, 6, 8, ?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 40' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0' font-family='sans-serif'><text x='10' y='28' font-size='18' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>2, 4, 6, 8, ?</text></svg>
questions.solution: The pattern adds 2 each time, so 8 + 2 = 10.

questions.question_text: What time is shown on the clock?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0' font-family='sans-serif'><circle cx='60' cy='60' r='50'/><line x1='60' y1='60' x2='60' y2='20' stroke-width='3'/><line x1='60' y1='60' x2='90' y2='60' stroke-width='2'/><text x='56' y='18' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>12</text><text x='95' y='64' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>3</text><text x='56' y='108' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>6</text><text x='15' y='64' font-size='10' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>9</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Clock with hour hand on 12 and minute hand on 3
questions.solution: Hour hand on 12 and minute hand on 3 means 12:15.

questions.question_text: A ladybug has 6 spots on one side. How many spots does it have on both sides together?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0' font-family='sans-serif'><ellipse cx='60' cy='45' rx='45' ry='30'/><line x1='60' y1='15' x2='60' y2='75'/><circle cx='35' cy='35' r='4'/><circle cx='45' cy='55' r='4'/><circle cx='25' cy='50' r='4'/><circle cx='40' cy='25' r='4'/><circle cx='20' cy='40' r='4'/><circle cx='50' cy='45' r='4'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Ladybug with 6 spots on left side
questions.solution: 6 spots plus 6 spots equals 12 spots.

questions.question_text: How many sides does a hexagon have?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0' font-family='sans-serif'><polygon points='50,15 85,35 85,75 50,95 15,75 15,35'/></svg>
questions.solution: A hexagon has 6 sides.

questions.question_text: How many circles are shown in the image?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 300 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0' font-family='sans-serif'><circle cx='30' cy='50' r='20'/><rect x='70' y='30' width='40' height='40'/><circle cx='140' cy='50' r='20'/><polygon points='185,70 210,30 235,70'/><circle cx='270' cy='50' r='20'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Mixed shapes with circles, a square, and a triangle
questions.solution: There are exactly 3 circles in the picture.

questions.question_text: Tom, Ben and Sam stand in a line. Tom is first. Sam is last. Who is in the middle?
questions.solution: If Tom is first and Sam is last, Ben must be in the middle.

questions.question_text: A basket has 5 apples and 8 oranges. How many fruits are in the basket?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0' font-family='sans-serif'><circle cx='15' cy='30' r='10'/><circle cx='40' cy='30' r='10'/><circle cx='65' cy='30' r='10'/><circle cx='90' cy='30' r='10'/><circle cx='115' cy='30' r='10'/><text x='135' y='35' font-size='16' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>+</text><circle cx='155' cy='30' r='10' fill='#e8e8e8'/><circle cx='180' cy='30' r='10' fill='#e8e8e8'/><circle cx='205' cy='30' r='10' fill='#e8e8e8'/><circle cx='230' cy='30' r='10' fill='#e8e8e8'/><circle cx='15' cy='60' r='10' fill='#e8e8e8'/><circle cx='40' cy='60' r='10' fill='#e8e8e8'/><circle cx='65' cy='60' r='10' fill='#e8e8e8'/><circle cx='90' cy='60' r='10' fill='#e8e8e8'/></svg>
questions.solution: 5 + 8 = 13 fruits.

questions.question_text: Which shape completes the pattern: square, circle, square, circle, ?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='10' y='15' width='30' height='30'/><circle cx='65' cy='30' r='15'/><rect x='90' y='15' width='30' height='30'/><circle cx='145' cy='30' r='15'/><text x='175' y='38' font-size='24' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>?</text></svg>
questions.image_description: Alternating square and circle pattern with a question mark
questions.solution: The pattern alternates square and circle. After circle comes a square.

questions.question_text: How many small squares make up this larger square?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='10' y='10' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='40' y='10' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='70' y='10' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='10' y='40' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='40' y='40' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='70' y='40' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='10' y='70' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='40' y='70' width='30' height='30'/><rect x='70' y='70' width='30' height='30'/></svg>
questions.solution: A 3 by 3 grid has 3 times 3 equals 9 small squares.

questions.question_text: Which number is missing: 10, 20, ?, 40, 50?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 280 40' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0' font-family='sans-serif'><text x='10' y='28' font-size='18' stroke='none' fill='currentColor'>10, 20, ?, 40, 50</text></svg>
questions.solution: The pattern increases by 10, so the missing number is 30.

questions.question_text: How many corners does the star have?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0' font-family='sans-serif'><polygon points='60,10 72,45 108,45 78,65 90,100 60,80 30,100 42,65 12,45 48,45'/></svg>
questions.solution: A five-pointed star has 5 outer corners (points).

questions.question_text: Lily is 7 years old. Her brother is 3 years younger. How old is her brother?
questions.solution: 7 minus 3 equals 4 years old.

questions.question_text: How many triangles are in this figure (including small and large)?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0' font-family='sans-serif'><polygon points='80,10 10,120 150,120'/><line x1='80' y1='10' x2='80' y2='120' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A large triangle divided by a vertical line from apex to base
questions.solution: Two small triangles plus the large one makes 3 triangles total.

questions.question_text: Anna has more marbles than Ben. Ben has more than Cara. Who has the fewest marbles?
questions.solution: Anna > Ben > Cara, so Cara has the fewest marbles.

questions.question_text: A farmer has chickens and cows. Counting all heads he gets 5, and counting all legs he gets 14. How many cows are there?
questions.solution: If all 5 were chickens that is 10 legs. The extra 4 legs mean 2 cows (each adds 2 legs).

questions.question_text: Which shape is NOT in the picture?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 300 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0' font-family='sans-serif'><circle cx='30' cy='40' r='20'/><rect x='70' y='20' width='40' height='40'/><polygon points='150,60 180,20 210,60'/><polygon points='240,25 280,25 280,55 240,55'/></svg>
questions.solution: The picture shows a circle, square, triangle, and rectangle but no star.

questions.question_text: How many dots are there in total?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0' font-family='sans-serif'><circle cx='20' cy='20' r='6'/><circle cx='50' cy='20' r='6'/><circle cx='80' cy='20' r='6'/><circle cx='110' cy='20' r='6'/><circle cx='140' cy='20' r='6'/><circle cx='170' cy='20' r='6'/><circle cx='20' cy='50' r='6'/><circle cx='50' cy='50' r='6'/><circle cx='80' cy='50' r='6'/><circle cx='110' cy='50' r='6'/><circle cx='140' cy='50' r='6'/><circle cx='20' cy='80' r='6'/><circle cx='50' cy='80' r='6'/></svg>
questions.solution: 6 + 5 + 2 = 13 dots total.

questions.question_text: A box has red, blue, and green balls. If you pick 3 balls, which result is NOT possible?
questions.solution: You pick only 3 balls, so 4 blue is impossible.

questions.question_text: How many squares (of any size) are in this 2 by 2 grid?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0' font-family='sans-serif'><rect x='20' y='20' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='60' y='20' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='20' y='60' width='40' height='40'/><rect x='60' y='60' width='40' height='40'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A 2 by 2 grid of small squares
questions.solution: 4 small squares plus 1 large square equals 5 squares.

questions.question_text: Three friends share 15 cookies equally. Then each eats 2 cookies. How many cookies does each friend have left?
questions.solution: 15 divided by 3 equals 5 cookies each. After eating 2, each has 5 - 2 = 3 left.
